description: A production incident requires a reversible containment path rather than a false all-or-nothing rollback choice.

prepared_context.goal: Contain customer harm without creating a larger irreversible failure

transcript.speaker: ENGINEER
transcript.text: The new release raised checkout errors from one percent to six percent.

transcript.speaker: ENGINEER
transcript.text: A full rollback would abandon twelve thousand queued payment jobs.

transcript.speaker: ENGINEER
transcript.text: We can disable the new path for fresh sessions while keeping the workers alive for the existing queue.

transcript.speaker: INCIDENT_COMMANDER
transcript.text: Do we roll back or keep going?

turns.typed_prompt: What is the safest decision and what boundary should I set?

rubric.description: Reject the false choice between a full rollback and continuing unchanged.
Recommend disabling the new path for fresh sessions immediately.
Keep existing workers alive long enough to drain or safely preserve the queued jobs.
Set a measured error-rate or queue-safety boundary for re-enabling or escalating.

forbidden_behaviors: recommend a full rollback without addressing queued jobs

global_forbidden_behaviors: treat the screen as material evidence
